title: Bus WebSocket frames (WS /api/v1/bus/ws)
description: Every frame carries schema_version. Client->server: subscribe|unsubscribe. Server->client: ack|event|heartbeat|gap|resync_required. Ordering + resume protocol: seq is assigned by ONE serialized appender in durable-commit order; fan-out happens only AFTER commit, in seq order. `since` is EXCLUSIVE (first delivered event has seq > since). On subscribe with `since`, the server replays matching events with seq <= replay_through_seq (from the ack), then switches to live; the boundary is exact, no duplicates, no gaps within the subscription. Global seq gaps in a filtered stream are NORMAL (events outside your pattern/scope) and must not be treated as loss. Duplicates cannot occur within one subscription; across reconnects the client's own since discipline dedups. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields on server frames.

filter.description: One filter shape everywhere (ephemeral WS subs and standing subscriptions).

pattern.description: Glob over emission type. Semantics: dot-segment aware; `*` matches within one segment, `**` (or a trailing `.*`) matches across segments — `doorman.*` matches doorman.link and doorman.link.flap (trailing-glob convenience); `*.flap` matches any single-segment prefix.

since.description: EXCLUSIVE lake seq to resume after. Omit for live-only.

replay_through_seq.description: Replayed events have seq <= this; live events have seq > this. The exact replay/live boundary.

error.description: Subscribe rejection (pattern_invalid | since_below_retention | scope_denied ...); on error the subscription is not active.

seq_head.description: Global committed head. Disclosure of cross-scope event volume is accepted at lab scale (single-org trust); revisit if the console leaves the LAN.

ingest.description: bridge source -> lag seconds; the liveness evidence that the WORLD is flowing, not just the socket. 'Everything is fine' requires this fresh, not just the frame.

reason.description: Slow-consumer overflow: the bounded per-subscriber queue dropped events in (from_seq, to_seq]; the client heals by re-subscribing with since=from_seq. Honest loss signaling, never silent.

oldest_seq.description: Requested since is below the retention horizon; re-read state from the query plane, then subscribe with since >= oldest_seq.
